title: Level 462
wikitext:
{{Rewrite|date=June 28, 2026|1=Needs lore expansion}} {{M.E.G Notice|reason=Confidential documents}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 462: "Moving Time"}} '''Level 462''' is the 463rd level of the Backrooms.{{Class Undetermined}} ==Description== {{Audio|align=left|file=metronomebackrooms133.mp3|volume=0.2}} '''Level 462''', or Moving Time, is a large, dimly lit, and architecturally distorted basement with a constant clicking sound present, resembling a storage unit for someone who's recently moved in. The level is furnished by various basement objects, such as chairs, tables, boxes containing family records, and lights, and stretches for several miles. The sheer number of these objects and their seemingly random distribution throughout this level have been noted by many. These objects are not stationary; they appear to constantly glitch into other places. Some have speculated that the objects are connected to [[The Broken]], but this remains unproven and unlikely. The clicking sound at 60 BPM remains constant under all circumstances. This clicking sound is an annoyance to wanderers, bringing them into submission and invoking fear. Studies show that at least half of the deaths in the level were connected to the clicking sound. Wanderers in '''Level 462''' often feel uninvited. This feeling has been studied, and it has been revealed that it is associated with guilt and shame. In addition, the level is known to have even more mental effects than that. Through several experiments, it has been discovered that the level tends to scan the mind of every wanderer and adjust the difficulty accordingly. By using feelings, it targets wanderers who are the most confident and the most afraid. It is not known how or why the level does this, so it is extremely advised to be cautious when entering it. Distorted architecture is common at this level. Tables, chairs, and boxes are of irregular sizes and strewn across the level. This architecture is, at times, extremely dangerous. Invisible booby traps are set throughout the level, and while they are rare, they can do incredible harm to wanderers. Due to this apparent hazard, most wanderers will not venture that far into the level, and so there is limited information about the layout of the level. Studies show that, on average, there are two booby traps for every mile in the level; however, the level adjusts this based on mental state.{{Blurquote|quote-text=60 bpm...|person=James B.}} ===Noclipping=== This level is known for its strange noclipping property. Normally, it has an isolation effect, preventing wanderers from being in the same copy of the level. However, several groups discovered that two or more wanderers could noclip simultaneously at the same location and remain in the same copy, defying the isolation effect. M.E.G tried to capitalize on this strategy in late 2009; however, this ambition would lead to the Moving Time incident. ===Moving Time Incident=== {{POI|name=James B.|seen=M.E.G base Zeta (Level 898)|location=Level 0-8, Level 898, The Void, Dead|status=Missing, Presumed Dead}}On December 28th, 2009, an M.E.G exploration group consisting of 20 scientists and 17 other M.E.G agents was launched into '''Level 462''' with the goal of colonizing the level. The group was swayed by a rumor that setting up a base would be easy. The expedition quickly turned sour, due to the noclipping going wrong. All 37 M.E.G agents attempted to enter the same wall, but tripped over one another. 14 agents ended up in the void; 6 in a separate copy of '''Level 462'''; and 2 in [[Level 0]], where they would later die for lack of resources. Although the remaining agents managed to reach the level, they were quickly overwhelmed by a [[Entity 5|Clump]]. The remaining agents scrambled to noclip out of the level; however, this attempt was ill-fated as well. In the end, every M.E.G agent except the leader (James B.) either ended up dead, in [[The Void]], or in [[The Whole]]. James B. tried to warn M.E.G about the level; however, his claims about the Clump were dismissed. He was later kicked out of M.E.G for cowardice. The group would later try to hunt James down, claiming he was planning to disrupt one of their operations on [[Level 1]]. It was later discovered that someone from M.E.G was jealous of James and deliberately ruined his operation. ==Entities== The existence of entities in the level has been pondered by many who have set foot in it. After much research, M.E.G and other organizations have still not been able to give a conclusive answer. The one surviving M.E.G agent from the moving time incident (James B.) claimed that his group was attacked by a [[Entity 5|Clump]]. Another incident involved a wanderer allegedly coming across a [[Entity 10|Skin Stealer]]. Most people who have gone to '''Level 462''' have concluded that entities do exist, but only appear rarely and discreetly. It has been noticed that groups are more likely to be attacked than wanderers.{{M.E.G Notice|Entity Notice|reason=Provoking the level could be the cause of entities appearing.|notice=Do not by any means, provoke the level.}} ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the inherent isolation effect, it is impossible to create a regular colony or outpost. But due to the noclip strategy mentioned earlier, it is possible for multiple people to noclip into the same copy of the level, rendering a colony or outpost remotely possible. However, no groups have been able to create one so far. ==Complete Timeline of the Level== This level is infamous due to many groups' failures to properly explore or colonize the basement, despite numerous expeditions. The level has gained a status of groups competing to gather the most information from of it. '''May 12, 1971''': This day was the first confirmed day of the existence of '''Level 462'''. '''January 8, 1979''': The first wanderer entered '''Level 462'''. '''February 16, 1981''': The first confirmed expedition into '''Level 462''', led by 20 wanderers, commenced. The wanderers successfully extracted some information from the level, but 11 went missing on the way back. '''July 6, 1982''': The first [[Backroom Colonists]] expedition commenced. All 5 members made it back safely. '''August 2, 1982''': A second [[Backroom Colonists]] expedition commenced. All 5 members survived, but 1 deserted on the way back. '''December 18, 1982''': A third and final [[Backroom Colonists]] expedition commenced, this time with 12 members. This time, the expedition was a disaster, as 11 of the 12 members fell into the void while noclipping out of the level. '''February 15, 1991''': The [[Entity Research Committee]] conducted an entity existence test. '''February 16, 1991:''' A group of former [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]] scientists explored the level. The leader of the expedition claimed it was no more than a "waste of a level number." '''May 23, 1993''': The secret "stairs" exit was discovered. '''March 17-23, 1996''': Many groups flocked to this level; however, interest quickly dwindled. '''December 28, 2009''': The Moving Time Incident occurred on this date. '''April 4, 2023''': A new wave of expeditions commenced on this date by various groups. Out of 432 total explorers, nearly 150 casualties were reported. Some expeditions were called off starting on June 27, 2023, with the rest ending by March 2, 2024.{{POI|name=John K.|status=Dead|location=N/A|affiliations=M.E.G (Low or No Rank)|seen='''Level 462'''}}
Date: December 6, 1987 From: John K. To: Tommy S. Subject of Letter: '''Level 462''' I hope you are doing well on Level 8. I do not know where I am, but it seems to be a basement similar to one of my Frontrooms houses. For some reason, I feel kind of lonely, nostalgic, and paranoid at the same time. I am with several friends, so I will luckily not be facing this basement on my own. We have brought several packs of food and supplies and are ready to defend ourselves if the plan goes awry. We plan to study this level in depth and report to M.E.G, so they can make a decision on whether to explore this level or not. I plan to vehemently suggest M.E.G not to explore this place, however I have a feeling that they will still do anyway. We are still working on finding more evidence to support the existence of entities. We don't believe that entities exist, however it doesn't hurt to check, right? I wish to go back to Level 898 immediately. Please tell Tony to say hello to the Level 898 base for me. Also, tell Tony to give me a good report. I want to be able to join M.E.G as a scientist. Sincerely, John '''The letter was publicized by a friend of John, who found it next to him after escaping.''' '''John himself was never seen again. He was posthumously admitted to M.E.G as a scientist.'''
==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== * The emergency exit at the back of [[Level 461]]'s visitors' center leads here when Level 461's Clear Skies event is not occurring. * Entering a small hole in the Comfort Rooms on [[Level 859]] will lead here. * Finding a door in a naturally dark room on [[Level 27.3]] will lead here. ===Exits=== * Noclipping may lead to [[Level 0]], but this method is exceedingly risky and has a chance to bring you to [[The Void]]. * Noclipping into a flight of stairs will take you to a [[Level List|random level]]. * Finding cave-related documents will take you to [[Level 620]]. * Opening a drawer with a paper of a smiler may take you to [[Level !]], but there's a chance to end up in [[Level 68]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Class Undetermined]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[zh:Level 462]]
